steps.title: Identify the main verb and subject of the relative clause
steps.content: The main verb is encourages a broader rethinking of British Romanticism itself. The subject is whose work. Between them comes a description involving the richness of Browne's poems and the poet's historical importance.

steps.title: Apply the convention for modifying phrases between subject and verb
steps.content: A participle can modify the subject of a clause without becoming a second independent verb. A finite verb in this position would create a run-on error—two independent verbs with no conjunction. The participle highlighting correctly modifies whose work while encourages stands as the main verb of the clause. That matches choice B — B is correct.
